pr-evidence/databricks-stage-and-submit/.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5/high reviewer Copernicus found that staged submit could partially upload before later artifact validation failed and that the README strict example omitted the DBFS plan artifact; both were fixed with regression coverage and doc updates.
verification: python -m pytest -q tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_repository_hygiene.py -> 120 passed before reviewer fixes
installed-wheel fake-opener smoke for stage_and_submit_databricks_run and cachet-databricks-runs stage-and-submit --help -> passed
what_changed: Added stage_and_submit_databricks_run and cachet-databricks-runs stage-and-submit for staging small DBFS artifacts before Databricks runs/submit.
Added an optional guard that fails before upload unless every dbfs:/ URI in the submit payload has a matching staged artifact destination.
Prevalidates all declared artifact files, DBFS destinations, and DBFS put payload sizes before the first upload request, then emits one artifact/upload plus submit provenance sidecar.
pr-evidence/databricks-stage-and-submit/.why: The provider-backed g6 probe needs a lower-risk env-auth launch command that stages runner/wheel artifacts and submits the payload without splitting provenance across manual steps.
